summary: Replaced Mr. Game & Watch motion table magic constants with named motion-flag combinations and named FtMoveId values, while also normalizing several fighter motion-state move-id encodings from multiplication by `(1 << 24)` to left shifts. The main cleanup introduced `ftGw_MotionFlags` in `ftGameWatch/forward.h` and used those names throughout `ftGw_Init_MotionStateTable`, making Game & Watch attack, landing, and special-state definitions much more readable without changing apparent table semantics.
what_changed: Added `ftGw_MotionFlags` to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftGameWatch/forward.h`, deriving named Game & Watch flag groups from existing `FtStateChange_*` bits.

Replaced hex motion-flag constants in `ftGw_Init_MotionStateTable` with names such as `ftGw_MF_Attack11`, `ftGw_MF_Attack100`, `ftGw_MF_Landing`, `ftGw_MF_SpecialN`, `ftGw_MF_SpecialAirS`, and `ftGw_MF_SpecialAirLwCatch`.
Replaced Game & Watch move-id hex constants with `FtMoveId_* << 24` expressions, preserving extra packed bits such as `(1 << 22)` and `(1 << 23)` where present.

Renamed several generic `FtMoveId_UnkXX` enum entries in `src/melee/ft/forward.h` to known semantic names: `FtMoveId_Attack11`, `FtMoveId_Attack100`, `FtMoveId_AttackLw3`, `FtMoveId_AttackS4`, `FtMoveId_AttackAirN`, `FtMoveId_AttackAirB`, and `FtMoveId_AttackAirHi`.
Updated Link and Young Link/CLink side-smash-2 motion entries from `FtMoveId_Unk10` to `FtMoveId_AttackS4`.
Normalized many fighter motion-state table entries across Donkey, Dr. Mario, Falco, Fox, Ganon, Link, Mario, and CLink from `FtMoveId_X * (1 << 24)` to `FtMoveId_X << 24`.
Changed one Game & Watch death/init part-index literal from `0xAU` to decimal `10` in `ftGw_Init_OnDeath`.
smart_moves: Moved recurring Game & Watch flag compositions into a forward header enum instead of scattering decoded hex values through the init table.

Built higher-level Game & Watch flag names compositionally, for example deriving air/special variants by OR-ing base flags with `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`, `FtStateChange_PreserveGfx`, or `FtStateChange_PreserveFastFall`.
Kept packed move-id fields explicit as shifts (`FtMoveId_* << 24`), which documents that the move id occupies the high byte better than multiplication by `(1 << 24)`.
Renamed only move ids that the diff directly justified from known Game & Watch/common attack states, while leaving still-unknown entries as `FtMoveId_UnkXX`.
Applied the shift-style cleanup consistently to neighboring fighter motion tables, reducing style drift around `MotionState` initializers.
decomp_lessons: For packed motion-state fields, prefer symbolic bit shifts and named bit flags over raw hex constants once the field layout is understood.
When a fighter table reveals common move-id meanings, promote those names in the shared enum so other fighters can immediately reuse them.
Character-specific motion flags can live in that character's `forward.h` when they are needed by init tables and can be expressed using shared `FtStateChange_*` bits.

Use compositional enum values for state-change flag groups; this makes relationships between ground, air, landing, attack, and special variants visible and reduces errors when checking bit preservation behavior.
Small cross-fighter mechanical cleanups are useful when they enforce the same representation convention without attempting to infer unrelated unknown semantics.
naming_conventions: `ftGw_MF_*` is used for Mr. Game & Watch motion-flag groups.
`ftGw_MS_*` remains the naming pattern for Mr. Game & Watch motion-state IDs.

`FtMoveId_Attack11`, `FtMoveId_Attack100`, `FtMoveId_AttackLw3`, `FtMoveId_AttackS4`, `FtMoveId_AttackAirN`, `FtMoveId_AttackAirB`, and `FtMoveId_AttackAirHi` replace corresponding generic `FtMoveId_UnkXX` names where meanings were identified.
Unknown move ids remain named `FtMoveId_UnkXX`, avoiding over-naming unsupported by evidence.
Motion flag names describe semantic groups and variants, e.g. `ftGw_MF_SpecialAirLwCatch` and `ftGw_MF_LandingAirHi`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Representing `FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24)` as `FtMoveId_* << 24` preserves the packed constant while expressing the field position more directly.
Hex table constants like `0x002C0201` and `0x02800000` can be decoded into OR-ed `FtStateChange_*` flags and shifted `FtMoveId_*` values without changing initializer values.
Extra packed flags in the move-id field were preserved as explicit ORs, such as `(FtMoveId_AttackAirN << 24) | (1 << 22) | (1 << 23)`.
No reviewer discussion or build/matching output was present in the provided PR slice, so matching impact beyond equivalent constant expression cleanup is not evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue renaming `FtMoveId_UnkXX` entries to semantic move IDs.
Search for remaining `* (1 << 24)` motion-state initializers that can be converted to `<< 24`.
Search for other fighter init tables with raw hex `FtStateChange` flag constants suitable for character-specific `*_MF_*` enums.
Check whether bits `(1 << 22)` and `(1 << 23)` in MotionState move-id fields later received named constants.
